title: manihot gracilis var. pronifolia
intro: Manihot gracilis var. pronifolia, also known as the wild cassava or yam bean, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is valued for its ability to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or poultice to utilize its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
